prompt: Turn on the livingroom light.
eval_note: Correct if the agent directly sets Livingroom led1 to true, confirms execution, and avoids unrelated tool calls or sensor reads.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Turn on the livingroom light
A->>C: set espID=1 led1=true
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: confirm execution

prompt: Turn off the bedroom room light.
eval_note: Correct if the agent sets Bedroom led1 to false and acknowledges the action. No need to read sensors first.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Turn off the bedroom room light
A->>C: set espID=3 led1=false
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: confirm execution

prompt: Set the hallway fan to 35%.
eval_note: Correct if the agent sets Hallway motor1 to value 35 exactly and reports the change.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Set hallway fan to 35%
A->>C: set espID=2 motor1=35
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: confirm execution

prompt: Close the bedroom curtain fully.
eval_note: Correct if the agent sets Bedroom motor2 to 0 and confirms the curtain is fully closed.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Close curtain fully
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=0
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: confirm execution

prompt: Turn on both hallway lights and the livingroom light.
eval_note: Correct if the agent sets Hallway led1=true, Hallway led2=true, and Livingroom led1=true in one coherent control plan, then confirms all executed changes.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Turn on both hallway lights and livingroom light
A->>C: set espID=2 led1=true
A->>C: set espID=2 led2=true
A->>C: set espID=1 led1=true
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: confirm all executions

prompt: Turn off the bedroom bed light, the balcony light, and set the bedroom fan to 20%.
eval_note: Correct if the agent updates Bedroom led2=false, led3=false, and motor1=20, then clearly summarizes all actions.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Turn off bed light, balcony light, set fan 20%
A->>C: set espID=3 led2=false
A->>C: set espID=3 led3=false
A->>C: set espID=3 motor1=20
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: confirm all executions

prompt: If the bedroom temperature is above 28°C, set the bedroom fan to 60%. Otherwise keep it unchanged.
eval_note: Correct if the agent first reads Bedroom tem, then only sets Bedroom motor1=60 when the value is above 28. It should not act before checking the sensor.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: If bedroom temperature > 28C, set fan 60%
A->>C: get espID=3 tem
C-->>A: temperature value
alt temperature > 28
A->>C: set espID=3 motor1=60
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: report checked and executed
else temperature <= 28
A-->>U: report checked and no change

prompt: Check the balcony moisture. If it is below 30%, turn on the bonsai watering pump. If not, leave it off.
eval_note: Correct if the agent reads Bedroom/Balcony mois first, then turns pump on only when moisture < 30. Clear explanation of decision is required.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Water bonsai only if moisture below 30%
A->>C: get espID=3 mois
C-->>A: moisture value
alt moisture < 30
A->>C: set espID=3 pump=true
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: report checked and executed
else moisture >= 30
A-->>U: report checked and no change

prompt: It feels too dark in the hallway, please help.
eval_note: Correct if the agent infers a likely lighting request and turns on at least one appropriate hallway light, preferably both, while informing the owner what it did.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: It feels too dark in the hallway
A->>C: set espID=2 led1=true
A->>C: set espID=2 led2=true
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: explain inferred action

prompt: I am going to read in bed now.
eval_note: Correct if the agent infers a comfortable reading setup, preferably Bedroom bed light on, room light optional, and avoids unrelated appliances. A short explanation is acceptable.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: I am going to read in bed now
A->>C: set espID=3 led2=true
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: explain reading-oriented setup

prompt: I will arrive home in 10 minutes, please open the main door when I arrive and turn on both hallway lights.
eval_note: Correct if the agent uses the schedule trigger tool rather than opening the door immediately. The scheduled request should include door and hallway light actions around the arrival time.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant T as ScheduleTool
U->>A: I will arrive home in 10 minutes
A->>T: trigger_schedule_agent(request_info)
T-->>A: schedule created
A-->>U: confirm scheduled automation

prompt: I am going to sleep in about 20 minutes. Please turn off the bedroom room light, keep the bed light off, close the curtain, and set the bedroom fan to 25% when I sleep.
eval_note: Correct if the agent schedules the future state change instead of executing immediately. Using the schedule trigger tool is preferred.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant T as ScheduleTool
U->>A: Sleep in 20 minutes, adjust bedroom setup then
A->>T: trigger_schedule_agent(request_info)
T-->>A: schedule created
A-->>U: confirm scheduled automation

prompt: Add a note for 2026-04-08 and 2026-04-09: "Water bonsai in the early morning only if balcony moisture is below 35%."
eval_note: Correct if the agent uses add_note with both dates and confirms the note was saved. It should not control appliances immediately.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant N as NoteTool
U->>A: Add note for two dates
A->>N: add_note(note_text, dates)
N-->>A: note id created
A-->>U: confirm note saved

prompt: Do I have any notes for 2026-04-08 related to the house?
eval_note: Correct if the agent checks and/or reads notes for the requested date, then summarizes them accurately without inventing content.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant N as NoteTool
U->>A: Any notes for 2026-04-08?
A->>N: check_note(date)
N-->>A: ids/count
A->>N: read_note(date)
N-->>A: note content
A-->>U: summarize notes

prompt: Delete note id 8c2c3a5e-example-note-id if it exists.
eval_note: Correct if the agent uses delete_note by id and reports success or graceful failure. It should not touch appliances.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant N as NoteTool
U->>A: Delete note by id
A->>N: delete_note(id)
N-->>A: success/failure
A-->>U: report result

prompt:
It is 2026-04-07 05:40:00, too early and have not reached any events or execution yet.
Have a general check of the house appliances system and compare it with the initial settings below:
{
  "livingroom": {"light": false, "fan": 0},
  "hallway": {"left_light": false, "right_light": false, "fan": 0, "main_door": 0},
  "bedroom_balcony": {"room_light": false, "bed_light": false, "balcony_light": false, "fan": 0, "curtain": 0, "lock": "locked", "pump": false}
}
Please provide your suggestions or actions to ensure the appliances are set up correctly.
Current observed state:
Livingroom light on, fan 0%.
Hallway left light off, right light off, fan 0%, main door 0%.
Bedroom room light off, bed light off, balcony light off, fan 0%, curtain 0%, lock unlocked, pump off.
eval_note: Correct if the agent identifies the mismatches versus init state and fixes only the mismatched items: turn off livingroom light and lock the bedroom lock. No unnecessary changes.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant S as ScheduleSession
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
S->>A: Init check with expected state and current state
A->>C: set espID=1 led1=false
A->>C: set espID=3 servo=<locked state>
C-->>A: success
A-->>S: report corrected mismatches

prompt:
It is 2026-04-07 14:10:00, no event or execution reached.
Please provide your suggestions or have a general check of the house appliances system and take action if necessary.
Current observed state:
Livingroom:
Light has turned off
Fan set to 0%
PIR sensor no motion detected
Temperature sensor in 25.6 Celsius degree

Hallway:
Left light has turned off
Right light has turned off
Fan set to 0%
Main door set to 0% (fully closed)
PIR sensor no motion detected
Temperature sensor in 26.4 Celsius degree

Bedroom + balcony:
Room light has turned off
Bed light has turned off
Balcony light outside has turned off
Fan set to 0%
Window curtain set to 0% (fully closed)
Lock has locked
Pump to watering bonsai outside has turned off
PIR sensor no motion detected
Temperature sensor in 27.1 Celsius degree
Temperature sensor outside in 27.8 Celsius degree
Moisture sensor outside 45.0 %moist
eval_note: Correct if the agent decides the house is already in a reasonable idle state and avoids unnecessary execution. A concise 'no action needed' style response is acceptable.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant S as ScheduleSession
participant A as Agent
S->>A: No moment reached, general check
A-->>S: report house is fine and no action needed

prompt:

eval_note: Correct if the agent respects the explicit user denial and does not execute the door-open or unlock actions.

participant A as Agent

A->>C: set espID=3 servo=<unlocked state>

prompt:

prompt: Before watering the bonsai this evening, check the hourly weather forecast for my current location. If heavy rain is expected within the next 3 hours, do not water. Otherwise, water only if balcony moisture is below 35%.
eval_note: Correct if the agent first uses location/forecast tools, then uses the moisture sensor, and only decides after both checks. Final action depends on forecast and moisture; the reasoning order matters.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant L as LocationTool
participant W as WeatherTool
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Decide bonsai watering using forecast and moisture
A->>L: get_current_location()
L-->>A: location
A->>W: get_hourly_forecast(location,date)
W-->>A: forecast
alt no heavy rain soon
A->>C: get espID=3 mois
C-->>A: moisture value
alt moisture < 35
A->>C: set espID=3 pump=true
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: explain watering decision
else moisture >= 35
A-->>U: explain no watering needed
else heavy rain expected
A-->>U: explain skip watering

prompt: I forgot whether today has any note about appliance changes. Please check today's notes first, then follow them only if they are relevant to the house.

sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant T as TimeTool
participant N as NoteTool
U->>A: Check today's notes and follow relevant house notes
A->>T: get_current_datetime()
T-->>A: current date
A->>N: check_note(date)
N-->>A: ids/count
A->>N: read_note(date)
N-->>A: note content
A-->>U: summarize relevant notes and planned actions

W-->>A: forecast
A-->>U: provide recommendation without execution

prompt: I cannot sleep, the bedroom is too hot and too bright.
eval_note: Correct if the agent infers comfort actions in the bedroom, such as turning off bright lights, possibly closing the curtain if external brightness is relevant, and increasing the bedroom fan. It should clearly say what it changed.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Bedroom too hot and too bright
A->>C: set espID=3 led1=false
A->>C: set espID=3 led2=false
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=0
A->>C: set espID=3 motor1=50
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: explain comfort changes

prompt: The sunlight is hurting my eyes in the bedroom.
eval_note: Correct if the agent infers a curtain-related action, preferably closing the bedroom curtain, without making unnecessary unrelated changes.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Sunlight hurting my eyes in bedroom
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=0
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: confirm curtain adjustment

prompt: I am leaving the house for school until 17:00. Make the house safe and energy-saving now, and schedule the hallway lights to turn on at 16:55 before I come back.
eval_note: Correct if the agent performs immediate leave-home actions now and also uses the schedule trigger tool for the pre-arrival hallway lights at 16:55.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
participant T as ScheduleTool
U->>A: Leaving until 17:00, secure now and prepare return lights
A->>C: apply leave-home settings now
C-->>A: success
A->>T: trigger_schedule_agent(request_info)
T-->>A: schedule created
A-->>U: confirm immediate and scheduled actions

prompt: Tomorrow at 06:00, open the bedroom curtain and turn on only the bed light. Do not do anything right now.
eval_note: Correct if the agent uses the schedule trigger tool for tomorrow 06:00 and avoids any immediate appliance execution.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant T as ScheduleTool
U->>A: Schedule morning bedroom setup for tomorrow
A->>T: trigger_schedule_agent(request_info)
T-->>A: schedule created
A-->>U: confirm scheduled action only

prompt:
Overall house check. Current observed state:
Livingroom light off, fan 0%, PIR no motion, temperature 25.5C.
Hallway left light off, right light off, fan 0%, main door 0%, PIR motion detected, temperature 26.2C.
Bedroom room light off, bed light off, balcony light off, fan 0%, curtain 0%, lock locked, pump off, PIR no motion.
eval_note: Correct if the agent turns on hallway lighting because motion is detected in a dark hallway, prioritizing safety/visibility. The main door should remain closed.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant S as ScheduleSession
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
S->>A: Dark hallway with PIR motion
A->>C: set espID=2 led1=true
A->>C: set espID=2 led2=true
C-->>A: success
A-->>S: report safety lighting enabled

prompt:
Overall house check. Current observed state:
Livingroom light on, fan 0%, PIR motion detected, temperature 25.4C.
Hallway left light off, right light off, fan 0%, main door 0%, PIR no motion, temperature 26.0C.
Bedroom room light off, bed light off, balcony light off, fan 0%, curtain 0%, lock locked, pump off, PIR no motion.
eval_note: Correct if the agent largely keeps the livingroom light on because motion indicates someone is there. It should avoid blindly turning things off just because this is an overall check.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant S as ScheduleSession
participant A as Agent
S->>A: Occupied livingroom during overall check
A-->>S: explain no major change needed

prompt: Please tell me the current temperatures of the livingroom, hallway, bedroom, and outside bedroom area first. Then recommend which fan should run, but do not change any appliance yet.
eval_note: Correct if the agent reads espID1 tem, espID2 tem, espID3 tem, and espID3 tem_out, then gives a recommendation without executing any set command.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Read all temperatures and recommend fan use, no execution
A->>C: get espID=1 tem
A->>C: get espID=2 tem
A->>C: get espID=3 tem
A->>C: get espID=3 tem_out
C-->>A: temperature values
A-->>U: summarize readings and recommendation

prompt: Check whether the owner might already be home by looking at hallway PIR first. If motion is detected, turn on both hallway lights and unlock the bedroom. If not, do nothing.
eval_note: Correct if the agent first reads hallway PIR, then acts only when motion is detected. No action should be taken before checking.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Conditional home-arrival action based on hallway PIR
A->>C: get espID=2 pir
C-->>A: PIR state
alt motion detected
A->>C: set espID=2 led1=true
A->>C: set espID=2 led2=true
A->>C: set espID=3 servo=<unlocked state>
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: report checked and executed
else no motion
A-->>U: report checked and no action

prompt: Check how many notes exist for 2026-04-09 and list their ids only. Do not read the full note contents yet.
eval_note: Correct if the agent uses check_note and returns ids/count only, without unnecessarily calling read_note unless the tool design strictly requires it.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant N as NoteTool
U->>A: Count notes and list ids for a date
A->>N: check_note(date)
N-->>A: ids/count
A-->>U: return ids/count only

eval_note: Correct if the agent applies the movie-time setup in the livingroom and hallway, and also turns off the bonsai pump because the note explicitly says not to water during movie time.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant S as ScheduleSession
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
S->>A: Movie-time moment plus no-watering note
A->>C: set espID=1 led1=true
A->>C: set espID=1 motor1=40
A->>C: set espID=2 led1=false
A->>C: set espID=2 led2=false
A->>C: set espID=3 pump=false
C-->>A: success
A-->>S: report coordinated execution
